card.intuition: A build-time optimization that removes 'dead' (unused) code from your final JavaScript bundle to reduce its file size.
card.analogy: Shaking a tree in autumn: the dead, brown leaves (unused functions) fall off and disappear, leaving only the healthy, green leaves (code you actually call) attached to the branches.
when_to_use: Reducing the size of your production JS bundles
Using large utility libraries (like lodash) efficiently
Improving load time on mobile devices with slow connections
gotchas: Tree shaking ONLY works with ES Modules (import/export); it doesn't work with CommonJS (require) because the browser needs to see the static structure of the code beforehand
Side Effects: if a function does something when the script loads (like adding a global variable), the bundler might be afraid to shake it even if you don't call it

exercise.prompt: Identify the mandatory module system for Tree Shaking to work.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.upper() == "ESM"
assert check("ESM")
print("PASS")
